input.prompt:
My MSTest project has a .testsettings file that configures test deployment, timeout,
and a code coverage data collector. After upgrading MSTest packages, the settings
seem to be ignored. How do I migrate this configuration?

data.content: Your `.testsettings` file is a legacy format that **MSTest v3+ ignores**. You need to migrate it to a `.runsettings` file. Let me create that for you.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <RunConfiguration>
    <!-- Migrated from TestSettings Property "TestTimeout" -->
    <TestSessionTimeout>30000</TestSessionTimeout>
  </RunConfiguration>
  <MSTest>
    <!-- Per-test timeout (equivalent to the old TestTimeout property) -->
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
    <!-- AssemblyResolution / deployment is handled automatically in SDK-style projects -->
    <DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>
  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.SingleFileCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=b03f5f7f11d50a3a">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage>
            <ModulePaths>
              <Exclude>
                <ModulePath>.*tests.*\.dll$</ModulePath>
              </Exclude>
            </ModulePaths>
          </CodeCoverage>
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.reasoningText:
The user has a `.testsettings` file that configures:
1. Test deployment (AssemblyResolution with TestDirectory)
2. Test timeout (30000ms)
3. Code coverage data collector

They're using MSTest 3.8.0 on .NET 8.0. The `.testsettings` format is legacy and not supported by modern MSTest. They need to migrate to `.runsettings`.

Let me use the migrate-mstest-v1v2-to-v3 skill since it covers replacing .testsettings with .runsettings.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <RunConfiguration>
    <!-- Migrated from TestSettings Property "TestTimeout" -->
    <TestSessionTimeout>30000</TestSessionTimeout>
  </RunConfiguration>
  <MSTest>
    <!-- Per-test timeout (equivalent to the old TestTimeout property) -->
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
    <!-- AssemblyResolution / deployment is handled automatically in SDK-style projects -->
    <DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>
  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.SingleFileCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=b03f5f7f11d50a3a">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage>
            <ModulePaths>
              <Exclude>
                <ModulePath>.*tests.*\.dll$</ModulePath>
              </Exclude>
            </ModulePaths>
          </CodeCoverage>
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.content:
I've created `local.runsettings` that replaces your `.testsettings`. Here's what was migrated:

| `.testsettings` setting | `.runsettings` equivalent |
|---|---|
| `TestTimeout` property (30s) | `<MSTest><TestTimeout>` + `<TestSessionTimeout>` |
| `AssemblyResolution` / deployment | `<DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>` — SDK-style projects resolve assemblies automatically |
| Code coverage `DataCollector` | Updated to the v2.0 collector URI with a `<CodeCoverage>` configuration block |

**To use it**, either:

1. **CLI:** `dotnet test --settings local.runsettings`
2. **In the `.csproj`:** add `<RunSettingsFilePath>$(MSBuildProjectDirectory)\local.runsettings</RunSettingsFilePath>` to a `<PropertyGroup>` so it's picked up automatically.

You can safely delete `local.testsettings` — MSTest v3 will never read it.
